parameters.content:
# Setup :
---

1. **Open the Webhook node** 
('Post /workflow/magic/position/id')
2. Copy the **Production URL**
3. Go to the **'Magic Positioning' Http Request** Node
4. **Paste the URL**
5. Select your **n8n credentials** in the n8n nodes

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Locate the webhook node named "POST /workflow/magic/position/id".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Locate the webhook node named "Simple Webhook Response".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Vector Store Retriever" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "In-Memory Vector Store" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Question and Answer Chain" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (1)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (2)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (3)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (4)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (5)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (6)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (7)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (8)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Node (9)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Tool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Dummy Tool (1)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model (1)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update n8n Workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Magic Positioning IA2S" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get n8n Workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Magic Positioning" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
